title: Poecilanthe hostmannii
intro: Poecilanthe hostmannii, also known as "Pao de queixo," is a type of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Poecilanthe hostmannii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu.
taxonomy: Poecilanthe hostmannii is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes. It belongs to the subfamily Faboideae. The genus Poecilanthe is part of the tribe Dalbergieae. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and genetic characteristics. Further research is needed to confirm its classification within the Fabaceae family.
